title: Sonic Jelly Sculptor
description: A BoomBox emits bass waves that deform a soft-body jelly horse and jelly parrot sitting on a vibrating platform. The user adjusts frequency, amplitude, and wave direction to sculpt the creatures into target silhouettes, with full undo/redo and energy conservation governing deformation budgets.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load BoomBox.glb centered at the back of the scene, Parrot.glb hovering mid-air to the right, and horse.glb standing on a platform to the left. Both the parrot and horse must be treated as soft bodies: clone their geometry, make vertices mutable, and apply a spring-mass simulation where each vertex is a mass point connected to neighbors by damped springs. Use a MeshPhongMaterial with translucent jelly-like appearance (opacity 0.8, shininess 90, color tinting). The BoomBox acts as a sound wave emitter. Implement a UI panel with: (1) a frequency slider 1-10 Hz controlling wave oscillation speed, (2) an amplitude slider 0-1 controlling deformation magnitude, (3) a direction dial with 8 compass points aiming the wave cone, (4) a pulse button that fires a single bass burst, (5) a toggle to link/unlink parrot and horse deformation coupling so waves bounce between them, (6) undo and redo buttons storing up to 20 snapshots of all vertex positions. Enforce an energy conservation law: total deformation energy across both models is capped at a constant. Increasing deformation on the horse must proportionally reduce available deformation on the parrot and vice versa, shown via two energy bar indicators. When the coupling toggle is active, deformation waves propagate from one model to the other with a 0.5 second delay, creating chain reactions. Display a ghost wireframe target silhouette for each model; the user must sculpt both soft bodies to match within a tolerance, requiring at least 10-14 deliberate interaction steps across frequency, amplitude, direction changes, pulses, coupling toggles, and undo corrections. Add a match percentage readout comparing current vertex positions to target. Use three-point lighting with a warm key light, cool fill, and rim light. Camera starts at a 45-degree elevated angle. Animate the BoomBox speaker cone pumping visually when emitting. Render at 60fps with requestAnimationFrame.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  waveFrequency: number,         // current bass wave frequency in Hz (1-10)
  waveAmplitude: number,         // deformation magnitude scalar (0-1)
  waveDirection: number,         // compass angle in degrees (0-360, 8 discrete steps)
  couplingEnabled: boolean,         // whether parrot and horse deformation are linked
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // constant energy cap for all deformations
  horseEnergyUsed: number,         // current energy consumed by horse deformation
  parrotEnergyUsed: number,         // current energy consumed by parrot deformation
  undoStackSize: number,         // count of snapshots in undo history (max 20)
  redoStackSize: number,         // count of snapshots in redo history
  pulseFired: boolean,         // whether a bass burst was triggered this frame
  platformVibrationIntensity: number,         // vibration amplitude of the platform (0-1)
  simulationActive: boolean,         // whether soft-body physics simulation is running
}
